title: Tickflow merge policy must merge existing worktree branch commits
description: Dogfood run of mir showed worktree agents may commit directly on their branch before returning. mergeWorktreeBranch only commits staged changes and returns merged=true when there are no staged changes, so ticks 9yp/fy4/i5u were closed even though their branch commits were not merged into the controller branch. Fix merge policy to compare worktree branch with controller HEAD/merge-base and merge existing branch commits even when no new staged changes exist; never close a tick until branch commits are actually merged or explicitly no-op.
acceptance_criteria: If a worktree branch has commits ahead of controller but no staged changes, Tickflow merges those commits before closing; test/manual repro covers agent-created commits; no-op close only happens when branch has no diff/commits relative to controller.
closed_reason: Fixed mergeWorktreeBranch to merge existing worktree branch commits even when there are no staged changes by checking merge-base --is-ancestor before treating branch as already merged. Dogfood branches 9yp/fy4/i5u were manually recovered and merged.
